Fix RangeSelector: use totalSupply (not circulatingSupply) for priceUsd

mcapToTick() divides by totalSupply to compute ZEUS price → ticks.
The displayed minPriceUsd/maxPriceUsd must use the same supply to stay
consistent. circulatingSupply (from CoinGecko) differs from totalSupply
and caused the displayed token price to not match the actual tick range.

Make Inbox the default tab; restore last-played episode on relaunch

  to second position. Main content loads /inbox on initial page load.

- Session restore: when the Mini App is launched, app.js synchronously
  reads buzz-last-episode-id from localStorage and patches hx-get on
  #content before HTMX's DOMContentLoaded listener fires, so HTMX loads
  the player page directly — no flash of the inbox first.
  The saved ID is written whenever a new episode is loaded into the
  persistent audio element.

* fix: set font.NameFarEast alongside font.Name for Japanese text support (#22)

font.Name controls only Latin/ASCII characters in PowerPoint COM.
Japanese (and other CJK) characters are controlled by font.NameFarEast.
Setting font_name without also setting NameFarEast leaves Japanese text
visually unchanged, while the API falsely reports success.

Changes:
- text.py _apply_font_props(): add font.NameFarEast = font_name
  (fixes ppt_format_text, ppt_format_text_range, ppt_batch_apply_formatting)
- tables.py _set_table_cell_impl(): add font.NameFarEast = font_name
- text.py _get_text_impl(): add font_name_far_east field to each run

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: retry COM operations when PowerPoint is busy (issue #31) (#35)

* fix: retry COM operations on RPC_E_CALL_REJECTED / RPC_E_SERVERCALL_RETRYLATER

PowerPoint rejects COM calls with these HRESULTs when a modal dialog is open
(e.g. SmartArt picker, Save dialog). Add a retry loop in _com_worker so that
ALL operations — including the initial connect — automatically wait and retry
up to 5 times at 3-second intervals, giving the user time to dismiss the dialog.

Both HRESULTs indicate the call was rejected before execution, so retrying is
always safe (no risk of duplicate state changes).

Closes #31

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: auto-dismiss PowerPoint dialog via ESC on first RPC_E_CALL_REJECTED

On the very first COM busy error, activate the PowerPoint window and send
ESC via WScript.Shell.SendKeys to close any blocking modal dialog before
the retry kicks in. This makes recovery immediate in practice (the next
3-second retry almost always succeeds) without spamming ESC on subsequent
attempts.

ESC is safe — it cancels without committing any destructive action.
Failure to send ESC is swallowed silently; retry logic continues as normal.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use keybd_event instead of WScript.Shell.SendKeys for ESC

WScript.Shell.SendKeys resets Num Lock / Caps Lock / Scroll Lock state
before sending keystrokes, causing spurious Windows accessibility
notifications ("Num Lock Off"). Replace with win32api.keybd_event which
sends only the requested key with no toggle-state side-effects.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: propagate busy COM errors from _connect_impl without wrapping

GetActiveObject and Dispatch both catch pywintypes.com_error and previously
re-raised it as ConnectionError, bypassing _com_worker's retry loop which
only catches pywintypes.com_error. Now busy HRESULTs are re-raised directly
so the retry loop (and auto-ESC dismiss) handles them correctly.

Addresses Codex P1 review comment on PR #35.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: correct off-by-one in retry loop (range → range + 1)

range(_RETRY_MAX) gave _RETRY_MAX total attempts but only _RETRY_MAX-1
retries (4 retries = 12s). Change to range(_RETRY_MAX + 1) and update
the guard to attempt < _RETRY_MAX, giving the intended 5 retries = 15s.

Addresses Codex P2 review comment on PR #35.

presentation — equivalent to PowerPoint's "Set as Default Shape" command.

visibility/color/weight, and font name/size/bold/italic/color. All color
values accept '#RRGGBB' hex or theme names (accent1, dark1, etc.).

* chore: bump version to v0.6.2

* fix: preserve aspect ratio in ppt_add_picture when only one dimension is given (#92)

* fix: preserve aspect ratio in ppt_add_picture when only one dimension is given

PowerPoint COM's AddPicture sets the specified dimension but leaves the
other at its natural (DPI-derived) value, causing distortion. Fix by
inserting at natural size first, then scaling proportionally.

Closes #91

* fix: remove double-scaling in ppt_add_picture single-dimension case

Setting pic.Width triggers PowerPoint's LockAspectRatio to auto-scale
the height. The previous code then applied an additional manual scale,
causing the image to be rendered at the wrong (smaller) size.

Fix: rely on LockAspectRatio for single-dimension cases; unlock it only
when both width and height are explicitly provided.

* fix: explicitly set LockAspectRatio=msoTrue in single-dimension branches

Makes the implicit COM assumption explicit for defensive correctness,
as suggested in code review.

* feat: add crop-to-shape with automatic aspect ratio fitting (#94)

* feat: add crop_shape parameter to ppt_crop_picture

Adds crop-to-shape support equivalent to PowerPoint's
Format → Crop → Crop to Shape, via shape.AutoShapeType on msoPicture.

- crop_shape accepts a friendly name ('oval', 'diamond', etc.) or
  MsoAutoShapeType integer; 'rectangle' resets to normal display
- Returns crop_shape integer in the response so callers know current state
- Validates that the shape is a picture (type 11 or 13) with a clear error
- Both rectangular crop and shape crop are independent and combinable

Closes #93

* fix: handle numeric string crop_shape and wrap COM error

- Accept numeric strings like "9" as equivalent to integer 9
- Wrap shape.AutoShapeType assignment in try/except with a clear
  ValueError describing the valid range (1-187) on COM failure
- Add note to field description that crop_shape is always returned
  as an integer in the response

Addresses code review feedback on PR #94.

* fix: validate crop_shape before applying rectangular crop

Move crop_shape name resolution and validation before the CropLeft/Right/
Top/Bottom mutations so that an invalid crop_shape aborts cleanly
without leaving partial updates on the slide.

Addresses Codex review feedback on PR #94.

* feat: add crop_fit and corner_radius_pt to ppt_crop_picture

Add two new parameters to enable perfect circle/shape cropping from
non-square images:

- crop_fit='square': auto-adjusts the crop frame to 1:1 aspect ratio
  by calculating crop values from original image dimensions via
  ScaleWidth/ScaleHeight. Combined with crop_shape='oval', this
  produces a perfect circle from any aspect ratio image.

- crop_anchor (0.0-1.0): controls which part of the image is kept
  when crop_fit is applied. 0.0 = left/top edge, 1.0 = right/bottom.

- corner_radius_pt: sets corner radius in points when using
  crop_shape='rounded_rectangle'.

Also adds width/height to the response for better visibility of
the resulting shape dimensions.
